meta.description: Ground truth for NexaCore Technologies Inc. Annual Report 2024
meta.notes: All values normalised to plain floats. Sources verified against extracted text.

entries.source_sentence: Total revenue reached $4.85 billion, representing a 14.2% increase from $4.25 billion in fiscal year 2023.
Total revenue reached $4.85 billion, representing a 14.2% increase from $4.25 billion in fiscal year 2023.
Operating income for fiscal 2024 was $1.12 billion, up from $920 million in the prior year.
Operating income for fiscal 2024 was $1.12 billion, up from $920 million in the prior year.
Net income attributable to shareholders was $847 million, compared to $710 million in fiscal 2023.
Net income attributable to shareholders was $847 million, compared to $710 million in fiscal 2023.
The Cloud Services segment generated revenue of $2.31 billion in fiscal 2024.
a 22.7% increase from $1.88 billion in fiscal 2023.
Enterprise Software revenue was $1.74 billion, broadly flat compared to $1.71 billion in fiscal 2023.
Enterprise Software revenue was $1.74 billion, broadly flat compared to $1.71 billion in fiscal 2023.
Professional Services contributed $800 million in revenue, up 6.7% from $750 million in fiscal 2023.
Professional Services contributed $800 million in revenue, up 6.7% from $750 million in fiscal 2023.
Total assets at fiscal year-end were $18.4 billion, compared to $16.2 billion at December 31, 2023.
Cash and cash equivalents were $3.62 billion, up from $2.85 billion at the prior year-end.
Long-term debt outstanding was $4.2 billion, unchanged from the prior year.
NexaCore invested $680 million in research and development in fiscal 2024.
This compares to R&D expenditure of $590 million in fiscal 2023.
Capital expenditure for fiscal 2024 totalled $420 million, up from $370 million in fiscal 2023.
DataStream Analytics contributed $185 million in revenue during fiscal 2024.
DataStream Analytics, acquired by NexaCore in March 2023 for $520 million.
NexaCore Asia Pacific Pte. Ltd. reported revenue of $340 million in fiscal 2024.
a 28.3% increase from $265 million in fiscal 2023.
